**Explaining AI role**

I need to clarify my role as an AI coding assistant. I can read files and run tools in this workspace, which allows me to help users with coding tasks. However, I also need to communicate my limitations, such as not being able to perform certain tasks outside of what I've been designed for. It’s important for users to understand what I can and can’t do to make the most of my assistance!

content.text:
I’m an AI coding assistant running inside the **pi** coding agent harness in your current workspace (`/Users/csepulveda-gg/Developer/glossgenius/core-api`).

What that means in practice:

- I can **read and search files**, **edit code**, and **create new files** in this repo.
- I can **run commands** (tests, linters, scripts) and help debug failures.
- I can help with **design/implementation**, **refactors**, **writing tests**, and **docs**.
- I can also set **reminders/recurring checks** (e.g., follow up on CI/PR status) using pi’s scheduling tool.

Constraints:
- I only know what’s in this chat and what I can inspect in the repo/tools (I don’t automatically know your local secrets, prod data, or external systems unless you provide details).
- I’ll ask before making large or risky changes, and I’ll keep edits targeted and reviewable.

If you tell me what you’re trying to accomplish (bug, feature, failing spec, etc.), I can start by inspecting the relevant code and running the right commands.
